title: cynometra phaselocarpa
intro: Cynometra phaselocarpa, also known as the Burmese ironwood, is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia, particularly in countries such as Myanmar, Thailand, and Malaysia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Cynometra phaselocarpa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it is also used to make herbal remedies in the form of extracts and decoctions.
